Create at least ONE data visualization that addresses your research question(s). This is a required deliverable due with your project plan.

**Primary Goal:**
What story does your visualization tell? What specific question does it answer?

The visualization will answer: "What is the relationship between performance and audience attitude for Clark and Reese?" It will result of whether audience attitude’s relationship between performance regarding two athletes with different backgrounds and profile.

**Visualization Description:**
Describe what type of chart/graph you're creating and what it will show. (e.g., "Bar chart comparing frequency of X vs. Y," "Line graph showing change over time," "Word cloud of most common themes")

We will create a grouped bar chart showing the proportion of positive vs. negative comments for each combination of CC vs. AR~~)~~ and the likes for two types of posts when at different performance level.

**Design Rationale:**
Why did you choose this visualization type? How does it clarify your argument or make your data more understandable?

Bar chart is appropriate because:

\- It allows simultaneous comparison of audience attitude with different players under different performance situations in a single view.

**Verification Methods:**
How will you ensure your visualization accurately represents your data?

* Spot-checked calculations against source data. We randomly selected 200 data from the cleaned data set, the accuracy of AI identifying the correct emotion is up to 68.5 percent (137/200).
* Had groupmate review for accuracy
* Verified percentages/totals add up correctly


**The Visualization:**
Embed your visualization here (as an image) or provide a link to the file.

We will be providing several bar chart created by AntiGravity or other visualization app after the data was cleaned and analyzed.

**Brief Interpretation (2-3 sentences):**
What does this visualization show? What pattern or insight does it reveal?

![][image1]
This graph shows the counting of AR in different performance level. It demonstrates the similar pattern of the positive/negative ratio whether AR performance better (better=1) and worse (worse=0).

[image1]: data:image/png;base64,
